etymology.description: 泉(泉) (quán, "spring") + 厂(厂) (chǎng, "cliff") = a spring emerging from beneath a cliff -> origin
etymology.interpretation: 原 (yuán, "origin") combines the image of a water source 泉(泉) (quán, "spring") underneath a cliff 厂(厂) (chǎng, "cliff"). This depiction suggests the idea of a source or beginning, where water flows out naturally from the base of a cliff, symbolizing an origin point.

examples.chinese: 这是故事的原点(這是故事的原點)。
examples.english: This is the origin of the story.

examples.chinese: 我想知道事情的原因(我想知道事情的原因)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒ xiǎng zhīdào shìqíng de yuányīn.
examples.english: I want to know the reason behind it.

examples.chinese: 有时候(有時候)，我们需要回到原来的地方(我們需要回到原來的地方)。
examples.pinyin: Yǒu shíhòu, wǒmen xūyào huídào yuánlái de dìfāng.
examples.english: Sometimes, we need to return to the original place.
